programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the felines of Ryann and Derek, who do not get along - Buddy has resorted to being a full time bully to the more placid Lita. Plus, everything goes smoothly between two flatmates until one gets a cat named Princess Puffy Pants, which urinates on everything

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A year in the life of five slow-moving residents of the Aviarios Sloth Sanctuary in Costa Rica, a place dedicated to saving orphaned or injured sloths. Buttercup gets a new throne, a romance develops between two three-toed bradypuses, and the resident scientist gets to the bottom of the ugly business of sloth digestion

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: An emergency rescue puts sanctuary vet Marcello on the spot, a love triangle leaves bradypus Samantha marginalised, and the resident scientist puts sloth speed to the test

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Howie Mandel is the Brains Behind ATM
programme.desc: Howie Mandel wants a tank, but is struggling to make up his mind on the design, leaving Wayde and Brett to complete the build without any guidance

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Investigator Marlynn Barkley tries to save a dog that has been left for dead by its owners. Later, she makes an emergency dash to the hospital

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy deals with a tantrum-prone cat that lives in fear of the other two felines living in the same home. He also tutors a bouncer on how to handle his misbehaving cat

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy works with a feline that is literally afraid of its own tail, and meets up with a pair of guardians whose cat is constantly knocking things over

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Documentary following Texas game wardens as they fight crime and protect natural resources. In South Texas, a warden assists in releasing cold-stunned sea turtles back into the sea. A standard patrol at a fishing spot leads to a drugs bust and a manhunt

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff tries to save a dog kicked in the head by a horse, and tackles a neighbourhood overrun with feral cats. A pet ferret displays worrying symptoms

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and the team build a charming treehouse in the style of a rustic river shack along the banks of the Mississippi

programme.title: Speed of Life
programme.desc: Super-slow motion cameras capture the 100mph strike of a yellow eyelash viper, a howler monkeys evasion of predators, and a lizard that can walk on water

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: At the head of the bay, Atz Snr has wild game in his sights, while Atz Lee and Jane tackle a long-overdue task to secure their cabin for the winter

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns rush to build livestock shelters as a life-threatening cold front reaches the mountain. Later, the siblings are forced to make a risky journey

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: The gruesome past that was uncovered when a paranormal group investigated a familys horrifying encounters with a ghost. Plus, a paranormal team that encountered an unexplained entity in the house of a young homeowner

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton learns about wild and domestic cats, and discovers the identity of the most popular cat on television in Little Rock, Arkansas

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: A young woman on the west coast of America experiences the night life with a pair of tamandua anteaters. Plus, the story of a couple who share their home with hippos

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson builds a treehouse for the children of injured Marines. Cargo netting provides an exciting way to enter, with more nets making a bridge between sections.

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami
programme.desc: An investigator fights to save a sick dog that has been rejected by its owners, while a girl rescues a horse left without any food or water

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates Louises cat Pip, a one-eyed cerebral hypoplasia feline, which will not stop tormenting and attacking the owners other cat, Red. Plus, Erins moggy Autumn causes havoc by urinating in several places around the home, including the settee, beds, kitchen worktops, microwave, computer monitor and backpacks

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: In Mexico, the crew navigate a maze of crocodile and mosquito-infested waterways and lagoons in search of a rumoured paradise for fishing tarpon

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Field operations manager Stacie DaBolt investigates when a dog with extremely matted fur and a harness embedded in its skin is brought to the shelter

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Investigator Marisa McHugh and Field Operations Manager Stacie DaBolt confront the owner of six emaciated dogs suffering from mange

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John Fulton learns about wild and domestic cats, and discovers the identity of the most popular cat on television in Little Rock, Arkansas

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia heads out to the swamps to rescue a dog in need of medical attention and is shocked by the diagnosis. Earl teaches his son how to drive

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps a soft-spoken musician who feels abused by her cat and is ashamed she cannot figure out how to prevent her pets behaviour

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy meets a woman who is over-run with neighbourhood feral cats, animals she has been feeding and trying to contain by herself for 20 years

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Summertime in Texas gives the game wardens plenty to do as they handle a speeding jet-ski rider, drunk boaters, angry campers and a rowdy raccoon

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff visits Mexico to help desperate pets, and some reluctant patients at a monkey sanctuary, while the team catch and treat a pack of street dogs

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson builds an outside-in treehouse in Alaska, heating things up by installing a sauna in the trees

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Marvin is reduced to tears by the sight of a pit bull puppy, an encounter with a snake makes Seth a prisoner in his own home and arachnophobe Jahara refuses to open any windows

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Eivin, Atz Lee, Jane and Shane go on a group hunt for deer to fill their freezers as the Kilchers make any final preparations for winter

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The ranch faces another crisis when the water supply freezes. Elsewhere, the Wolfpack must problem solve to get the house back on track in time for the deep freeze

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A man is seduced by a female demon, so his wife desperately tries to save him from the dark side

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: Renowned bear handler Jeff Watson sets out on an adventure to teach his two 700lb grizzly bears, Bob and Screech, how to survive on their own in the wild

programme.title: Richard E Grants Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant explores the origins of the seven deadly sins, examining the dramatic effect they have. He explores the roots of envy - the resentful longing for other peoples possessions or qualities - and also investigates if it exists in the animal kingdom

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete creates a treehouse cinema, with loungers elevated into the trees to give a perfect view of the screen and food and drinks served by way of a pulley system

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami
programme.desc: Investigators seize 400 farm animals in one of the biggest operations of its kind in Miami, and also rescue an injured alligator

programme.title: Animal Cops Miami
programme.desc: A dramatic rescue to save a manatees flipper uncovers a secret, and investigator Casadevall hunts for poachers who have left several animals dead

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Truth About Len Deighton
programme.desc: An interview with the writer in which he talks about his many ventures, including espionage novels, cookbooks, military histories and the films which he has written and produced. With contributions by Michael Caine and Max Hastings. First broadcast in 2006

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack looks at structures that have been built for arts, leisure and entertainment purposes, including the Globe Theatre, the Victoria and Albert Museum and Blackpool Tower

programme.title: Cornwalls Red River
programme.desc: Poet John Wedgwood Clarke explores the waterway, which is home to some rare and resilient wildlife despite being heavily polluted by centuries of tin mining. Extracts from Johns poem Red River at the A30 Culvert reveal his mixed thoughts about the rivers toxic legacy

programme.title: All About Eve (1950)
programme.desc: An ageing stage star is flattered by the attention of an adoring fan, and decides to take the young woman under her wing as her personal assistant - unaware that her new protegee is really a ruthlessly ambitious, budding actor who will do all that it takes to steal the limelight. Oscar-winning drama, starring Bette Davis, Anne Baxter, George Sanders, Thelma Ritter, Marilyn Monroe and Celeste Holm

programme.title: Wogan
programme.desc: Terry Wogan is joined by American actress Bette Davis, who makes sure the host does not forget to mention her book. First broadcast September 14, 1987

programme.title: The Ipcress File (1965)
programme.desc: An intelligence agent investigating the kidnap and brainwashing of Britains most renowned scientists unpicks a complex chain of connections and high-level intrigue, while detecting evidence of double-dealing and counter-espionage. Spy thriller based on Len Deightons novel, starring Michael Caine, Sue Lloyd, Gordon Jackson and Nigel Green.

programme.title: The Cure at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of Robert Smith and his band and the unique song-writing talents they possess, with a collection of archive performances on BBC programmes. Featuring A Forest, Lovecats, Friday Im In Love, Close to Me and Boys Dont Cry

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Filmed without voiceover or music, this programme highlights the call of the birds at sunrise, as those living within Britains habitats burst into song and reveal the natural splendour of their distinctive chorus

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack looks at structures that have been built for arts, leisure and entertainment purposes, including the Globe Theatre, the Victoria and Albert Museum and Blackpool Tower

programme.title: Cornwalls Red River
programme.desc: Poet John Wedgwood Clarke explores the waterway, which is home to some rare and resilient wildlife despite being heavily polluted by centuries of tin mining. Extracts from Johns poem Red River at the A30 Culvert reveal his mixed thoughts about the rivers toxic legacy

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on July 9, 1999. Including performances from Blur, Melanie G, Shania Twain, Semisonic, and Atb

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast July 16, 1999. Including performances from Atb, Semisonic, Gary Barlow, Beverley Knight, and Ricky Martin

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates introduces performances by Stiff Little Fingers, Liquid Gold, Children of Tansley School, Sugar Minott, Spandau Ballet, Shakin Stevens, Stevie Wonder, the Nolans and Bucks Fizz, and a dance performance by Legs and Co. First shown on April 2, 1981

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Janice Long introduce the edition from April 2, 1987. Featuring performances by the Pogues and the Dubliners, Madonna, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Fine Young Cannibals, the Rainmakers, Herb Alpert, Whitesnake, Ruby Turner and Ferry Aid

programme.title: The Cure at the BBC

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The Cure play an intimate performance with a selection of classics, fan favourites and tracks from the album Songs of a Lost World. Recorded in front of a small audience at the BBC Radio Theatre in Londons Broadcasting House

programme.title: 6 Music Festival 2026
programme.desc: Highlights from the 2026 BBC Radio 6 Music Festival, which took place at independent venues across Greater Manchester. Featuring performances from Bloc Party, Courtney Barnett, The Horrors, Jacob Alon, Kelly Lee Owens, Wesley Joseph, Lynks, Yard Act and Mandy, Indiana

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is appalled when she spots a strange man wrapped in only a towel exiting Elizabeths house next door. Convinced that it is her moral duty to save her friend from this scandal, Hyacinth begins to plot ways of flushing the man out

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.sub-title: Does Your Mother Know Youre Out
programme.desc: Mother wants Timothy to paint the cupboard under the stairs before the man comes to read the meter. However, Timothy is more concerned with betting snide colleague Victor that he can take new library assistant Caroline for a cinema trip to see Gone With the Wind

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The team celebrate the wild stretch of water that is half mile lake which is home to Californian sealions, African hippos and native beavers with gorillas and monkeys living on the banks. Hamza Yassin is given special access to the park after the visitors have gone home so he can capture some of the wildlife living in the lake. Kate Humble joins the vets as they check up on Wilma the cheetah who has kidney disease. Megan McCubbin visits Chico the sloth to ensure the conditions in his enclosure match the ones he would be used to in the wild

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Illegal Blood Sports and Tractor Sabotage
programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, farmer Helen Smith has endured years of intimidation from illegal off-road bikers tearing across her land. Now, with her elderly mother living in fear after shots were fired at their home, police are determined to track down the gang behind the violence. In Hampshire, officers pursue men suspected of terrorising wildlife and wrecking farmland by taking part in the illegal blood sport of hare coursing. Plus, reporter Paul Colgan discovers how farmers in the Scottish Highlands doubling as on-call firefighters are using cutting-edge technology to protect their rural communities

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: How a gang illegally digging up roads to divert electricity to power dozens of cannabis farms was caught. Plus, the shoddy builder who took £35,000 from an unwitting victim. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: In a special 20th anniversary edition, Amanda Lovett, star of BBC hit show The Traitors, joins Tommy Walsh to look around a derelict flat in Buckinghamshire. Jacqui Joseph is in Norfolk to see a period property with bags of character. Plus, a delve into the Hammer archives with a clip in which Martin Roberts meets a lottery-winning couple

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and the teams shop for antiques in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, with experts Izzie Balmer and John Cameron offering expert advice. Later, they head to auction in Beverley, East Yorkshire. Danny also visits an exhibition about pioneering pilot Amy Johnson

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball prescribes ten minutes of sunshine a day for retired woodworker Neville, who has suffered a series of heart attacks. She explains how just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift mood

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Matt has built his wifes dream home and he has filled it with not only his collections but those of his late father. The bedrooms, garage and even the summerhouse are packed to the rafters with boxes filled with collectables. Its a dire situation for Paula, his wife of 25 years, who is desperate to clear her husbands clutter. The couples six daughters have threatened to throw everything in a skip, so Izzie Balmer has just one day to root out the treasures from the trash before its too late. Plus, JJ Chalmers uses a toothpaste hack to add value to damaged collectables

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A mother and son with a £350,000 budget return from overseas, hoping to find a classic Kent cottage. Denise Nurse discovers a project where bison have been introduced to woodland

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk travel around the UK fixing historical treasures, with this edition seeing them visit the Scottish Boat Building School in Irvine, North Ayrshire, to restore a three-piece canoe designed in the 1930s. Will also calls in to Barr Castle in Galston, East Ayrshire, to repair a windowpane with a hand inscription by Robert Burns

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which contestants Shauna, Andy, Doniya, Kathryn and Martin race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Rose Matafeo

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Angellica Bell present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents gather for the opening of Knight Fusion, Ian attempts to make amends, and Vicki and Ross think about wedding venues

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Reality game show with John Hannah as narrator. Five adventurous teams are pushed to the limit, battling extreme climates and harsh terrain in a 12,000km race from Palermo, Sicily, across Europe and Asia to reach Hatgal in northern Mongolia. Best friends Jo and Kush from Liverpool, siblings Katie and Harrison from Manchester, Molly and her father Andrew, cousins Puja and Roshni and in-laws Mark and Margo take on the challenge

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The teams are unleashed on the pet industry as they are tasked with designing a product and social media campaign to pitch to some of the UKs biggest retailers. With one candidate in the firing line, its a battle for a place in the final five

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Harry Clark Goes to Rome
programme.desc: The Traitors star goes on a deeply personal journey from Slough to Rome and Vatican City in search of answers about belief, identity and what it means to be a good Catholic in the modern world. Along the way, hes challenged on his lapsed habits, engages in traditional pilgrim practices and raises questions about the role of faith in modern Britain

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Central Leeds crew Nagina and Mo are called to a siege where police are currently negotiating with a man who is threatening to harm himself and the officers attending. They must be ready to enter the building to treat any potential casualties. Meanwhile, married crewmates Jen and Elliott are dispatched to a 61-year-old in Yeadon, who has had a suspected stroke, then treat a patient having a seizure at the train station

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble shares prey-eye view footage featuring a pride of lions. Hamza Yassin attempts to befriend a kangaroo, known as Brave, who is notoriously nervous around men

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fly-Tippers Get Caught in the Act
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, farmers take a stand against fly-tippers who have been blighting their land with van loads of dumped waste. In Northern Irelands Mourne Mountains, fast moving wildfires threaten grazing land and livestock, as exhausted firefighters and farmers join forces to bring the flames under control. In north Wales, reporter Paul Colgan meets Rhianon Bragg, a courageous survivor of domestic abuse whose terrifying ordeal on her rural smallholding exposed the hidden dangers faced by victims living in isolation

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The story of a van driver who called 999 after making a horrific discovery, leaving police to determine if he was telling the truth or covering a crime. Plus, the hunt to catch a right-wing extremist

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Jacqui and Frankie from The Traitors
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph recruits The Traitors star Frankie Rowan-Plowden to oversee the auction of a property in Kent and theres a cottage in Cornwall that has taken quirky to a new level. Martin Roberts is in Bristol to see a mid-terraced house

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Shepton Mallet, Somerset. Mother-and-daughter duo Bernie and Emma search for bargains with the help of Raj Bisram but find themselves reluctant to part with cash at first. On the blue team, friends Amy and Caroline are on the hunt for pottery with the help of Thomas Forrester

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Elaine and Paul in Mickleover, Derbyshire, love all things 1950s - be they cars, clothes or music. Elaine also loves fairies. On a budget of £3,000, Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull create a garden inspired by the rock and roll era that will also make the couples fairy-tale dreams come true

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ian H Watkins, along with his partner Tom, put property expert Denise Nurse through her paces as she attempts to find their perfect family home in the south Wales countryside where the Steps singer was born and raised. Away from the search, they all try their hands at sheep herding and visit a Welsh tea plantation

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which four pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Gethin Jones

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Abi Trotman recreates a miniature version of a 1980s east London school classroom for restaurateur Kim, capturing memories of a beloved teacher and the first real sense of safety and belonging. Presented by Sara Cox

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Angellica Bell present the topical magazine-style programme that showcases stories of all varieties from all over the UK, ranging from the comic to the tragic, as well as interviews with guest stars

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Iron Gran: Im in My 80s and Doing Triathalons
programme.desc: The comedian invites artist Caroline Pool to paint Eddie - aka Iron Gran - an inspiring 82-year-old who has completed eight Ironman triathlons. After losing her husband in her 50s, Eddie started running long distance to help her cope. At 60 she learned how to swim and at 72, she became the oldest British woman to ever complete an Ironman triathalon, 10-years later she shows no signs of slowing down. Caroline faces the dilemma, should she capture Eddies fun-loving energy or reveal something quieter and more reflective beneath the surface?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: A couple are targeted by a burglar who repeatedly sneaks back in to clean up without a trace. With several potential suspects, Humphrey must uncover who is out for revenge. Kelby tries to help a young teen held captive in a garden shed and Ester wrestles with a tension headache. Despite putting fostering on pause, Humphrey and Martha find the decision at the forefront of their minds

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Roy Wood Jr chairs the satirical current affairs quiz, with Armando Iannucci and Anushka Asthana joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the recent headlines

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Alex Murphy. Conor and Jock finally reunite after Jock makes a lucky escape from the Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor James McAvoy, who talks about his directorial debut, The Morning Show star Gugu Mbatha-Raw, discussing her crime thriller Fuze, actress, singer and producer Rita Wilson, chatting about her sixth studio album, and comedian Russell Howard, whos promoting his stand-up tour Dont Tell the Algorithm

programme.title: Wedding Crashers (2005)
programme.desc: Two party-mad bachelors make the most of the romantic atmosphere as they gatecrash weddings to pick up women. With a catalogue of tall stories to tell guests, the pair are regularly the hit of the events they attend - until one of them falls for a bridesmaid and they both learn a few things about love. Comedy, starring Owen Wilson, Vince Vaughn, Christopher Walken, Jane Seymour, Rachel McAdams and Isla Fisher

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor is becoming an increasing nuisance in Healy and Maireads house, and when Healy shares that hes going on a fishing trip to where he grew up, Mairead insists he bring Conor. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Theres a new fake Billy Murphy in town, and he steals Conors most prized possession - the engraved lighter given to him by his best friend Jock. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: After Conor and Jock are almost caught by Sergeant Healy following a disastrous bike theft, Mairead tires of the duos immature behaviour and demands change. To move matters along, she books them into a therapy session in Dublin, hoping it will imbue them with a sense of responsibility. A road trip is duly arranged, leading to bizarre encounters with a bed and breakfast owner, the therapist herself (Father Teds Pauline McLynn), and an unsuspecting pub crowd. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Theres a commotion in the housing estate, and Conor and Jock discover can-trailers - tearaways from other estates with open paint cans tied around bikes intent on destroying the rival estate. The lads stop their rivals, but the encounter leads to an argument and Jock is challenged to a boxing match - which he reluctantly agrees to. The duo soon discover his prospective opponent is a champion fighter, meaning Jock has to embark on an extensive training programme in order to sharpen his skills for the bout. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide its time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when theyre living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as hes about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall presents an edition showcasing the contenders that did not make it to the final 60

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels to Indonesia in search of three reptiles to add to his list of predators, including the Komodo dragon and the potentially lethal green pit viper

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: Six teenagers learn where their clothes have come from, travelling to India to see first-hand the textiles industry that churns out designer-wear for Western consumers like themselves. As the group experience life in these workshops, the stress of life where employees literally eat and sleep by their machines becomes too much for one - and that is before they learn about their wages

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The six teenagers learn, live and work in a backstreet sweatshop as they are given three days to produce 36 blouses between them among locals who each have to stitch 20 a day for just 25p a garment. The areas poverty leaves Richard struggling to cope, but that is nothing compared to the physical challenge of their next destination - Indias cotton fields

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock have grown older, but not any wiser. A madcap trip sees them on the wrong side of the law as theyre given a free holiday to Colombia from a Spanish-speaking man. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor takes a job at the fishmongers, still desperate to get back with his former flame Linda. Conors arch-enemy, Gavin Madigan, continues to taunt him. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: Documentary following the British-Gambian musician as he attempts to rebuild his life and career after prison, returning to live performance while still on probation, navigating curfews, travel restrictions and the risk of recall. He makes his comeback at the Glastonbury Festival, but touring in Europe hangs in the balance, and he faces a choice between breaking commitments and risking a return to prison

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May test-drive an Aston Martin DBS Convertible, a Ferrari California and a Lamborghini Gallardo Convertible through Romania, searching for the mountain highway built during the reign of the countrys former dictator Nicolae Ceausescu - allegedly the best road to drive on in the world. Actor Eric Bana is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Jock and Siobháns daughter Star is turning six and a birthday party is being organised. Orla Walsh however is having her kitchen redone, and asks Mairead to host the party. Coming-of-age comedy drama, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Six-year-old Star accidentally discovers the Billy Murphy mask, infuriating Sergeant Healy. Conor and Jocks genius plan to help Jock and Star have a video call is rumbled by Linda. Coming-of-age comedy drama, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley

programme.title: Murdered by My Boyfriend
programme.desc: Drama about a teenage girls romance with an older man who, although handsome and initially charming, soon begins to dominate every aspect of her life as his jealous and possessive nature gets out of control. Starring Georgina Campbell and Royce Pierreson

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: Six teenagers learn where their clothes have come from, travelling to India to see first-hand the textiles industry that churns out designer-wear for Western consumers like themselves. As the group experience life in these workshops, the stress of life where employees literally eat and sleep by their machines becomes too much for one - and that is before they learn about their wages

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall sets out to visit six continents in search of Earths 60 deadliest animals, with Ethiopia this weeks port of call

programme.title: The Mask (1994)
programme.desc: A timid bank clerk stumbles on an ancient mask, which endows whoever wears it with untold superhuman powers, unbridled self-confidence and an irresistible charm for the ladies. Unfortunately, he earns the enmity of a local gangster for daring to flirt with his girlfriend - and when the crook gets wind of the artefacts existence, he covets the power for himself. Fantasy comedy, with Jim Carrey, Cameron Diaz, Peter Riegert and Peter Greene

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set crime drama returns, and there is a double celebration as the show marks its landmark 100th episode and Commissioner Selwyn Patterson celebrates 50 years of service. However, the party is cut short when the boss is shot by a mysterious assassin and left fighting for his life. As the team members search for the culprit, will the police veteran survive? Starring Ralf Little, Don Warrington and Shantol Jackson

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The six youngsters experience the physical challenge of the cotton harvest, where they are shocked by the laborious process and the appalling standard of the living conditions, with the daily wage being just £1.20. Though they are in a dingy hovel with no amenities, for Georgina the most horrifying fact is that her Indian co-workers shop for clothes twice a year at most

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The teenagers experience life in the Mumbai city slums as they are set to work in segregated sweatshops barely a stones throw from the enticing glamour of Bollywood. One saving grace is that unlike the locals, they do not have to sleep at their sewing machines, but with children below the legal minimum working age with them, they finally agree that something has to be done

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet announces she is pregnant, much to Jonnys delight. Gaz leaps into action to foil Louises blackmail scheme, and Donna discovers selling the house is going to make her rich. Ralf Little and Sheridan Smith star

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny decides to prove his responsibility by learning to drive, but his efforts come to an abrupt halt when Louise holds his car hostage. Donna splits from Gaz and resolves to take revenge on Janet - but eventually decides to leave the country instead

programme.title: Murdered by My Father
programme.desc: A widowed father attempts to ensure his daughter is set up with the right husband. However, as her planned engagement approaches, she falls for a charismatic charmer and becomes caught in a conflict between two generations with differing values. Fact-based drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Kiran Sonia Sawar

programme.title: Blood, Sweat and T-Shirts
programme.desc: The six teenagers learn, live and work in a backstreet sweatshop as they are given three days to produce 36 blouses between them among locals who each have to stitch 20 a day for just 25p a garment. The areas poverty leaves Richard struggling to cope, but that is nothing compared to the physical challenge of their next destination - Indias cotton fields

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: Documentary looking at four of Britains 15 national parks, capturing the sights and sounds of these unique places and meeting the people who live and work there. The opening edition visits the New Forest, once a royal hunting ground for William the Conqueror and now a mosaic of open heathlands, ancient woodlands, coastline and picturesque villages. The park is home to ponies, deer, rare bird species and reptiles, while the traditional grazing practices of generations of commoners sustain this landscape, with their ponies and pigs roaming freely throughout the park

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from Northern Ireland are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to Northern Ireland. The dishes are judged by veteran judges Jean Delport and Sally Abe, and include a roast chicken inspired by Kenneth Branaghs adaptation of Murder on the Orient Express, as well two desserts inspired by the film Good Vibrations

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The judges are 2025s banquet winners Sally Abe and Jean Delport. Dishes include a red knotted brioche handkerchief as a homage to School for Good and Evil. Also, theres a tribute to director Lisa Barros DSa with barbecued monkfish served from a teapot

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When the circumstances of a crime novelists death eerily mirror the details of his final novel, Humphrey must uncover whether it was a coincidence or murder by design. The Lily Bond is caught adrift leaving Humphrey and Martha back home with Anne. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.desc: Chaquie helps Rachel get ready for an important event for Anna. On the way, Celine and Rachel stop by the Walsh house - only to discover Anna is missing. Claire drops Kate off to James, but their tense exchange leaves her questioning how their co-parenting arrangement is working. Also, after an argument between the sisters, a quiet moment of unity pulls them back together

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Roman Kemp suggests a reverse-moves approach to exercise, including walking downhill instead of up and lowering weights instead of lifting them, both of which could have significant health benefits

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse helps a couple who want a unique Sussex home, and the quirkier, the better. Her line-up includes some wow-factor properties, any one of which could be the mystery home

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don is busy with spring and planning ahead for summer. Hes doing a seed-sowing masterclass, cutting back Cornus and dividing ferns to put in the new Woodland Garden. Frances Tophill is exploring the tiny but expansive world of miniature daffodils at a nursery with hundreds of cultivars. And in Llanelli, Sue Kent meets a gardener who has set up her own seed library, helping to preserve varieties of flowers and edibles which flourish in local Welsh soil and beyond

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: Clive visits Bologna, considered by many to be Italys food capital, where he leans to make perfect pasta and finds out why an osteria - an Italian wine bar - is superior to a pub. But its not all food and drink as he also dons his hard hat and climbs some scaffolding to learn the history of an 800-year-old tower

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: Zoologist George McGavin concludes his exploration of the primate world, considering their capacity to use tools and solve problems, something he claims sets them apart from most other families on the planet. By way of demonstration, the presenter goes to orang-utan school in Sumatra, visits Thailand to see long-tailed macaques floss their teeth with human hair and use tools to open oysters on the beach, and reveals how Ugandan chimpanzees pass on cultures and customs down the generations. He also meets a resourceful bonobo in the States that can order its own picnic on the phone and build a fire, on which it then toasts marshmallows

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts make a return visit to Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire, where they value a 17th-century wine bottle and a collection of letters, photographs and medals belonging to three suffragettes from Glasgow. Among the other treasures are a Victorian dressing case which, despite having travelled from England to Brazil and Guernsey, remains in pristine condition. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: An ale jug and an unusual gold scarab are among the items examined by Tracy Martin and Charlie Ross at the Sheldonian Theatre in Oxford. Presenter Paul Martin visits the drawing and print room at the Ashmolean Museum

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Amid Belfasts buzzy food scene, Rick tucks into Filipino-style Sunday lunch. On Northern Irelands Atlantic coast, he meets an award-winning chef, with a no waste approach, who turns fish offal and under-used species such as dog fish into stunning dishes. Rick also makes his first Filipino dish, and, after witnessing the dry-ageing of some special Irish beef, cooks beef and Guinness pie

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from Northern Ireland are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to Northern Ireland. The dishes are judged by veteran judges Jean Delport and Sally Abe, and include a roast chicken inspired by Kenneth Branaghs adaptation of Murder on the Orient Express, as well two desserts inspired by the film Good Vibrations

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Callum needs to haggle hard to get a good price for his calves from a mainland buyer, while Zaras morning cattle check brings devastating news. Andrew feels under pressure to make the farm pay more so his dad can take a step back, but right now its all hands to the pump as a pregnant Gayle has been signed off work. Noah wants a future in farming, but he needs to balance lambing with exam revision.

programme.title: Kegworth: Flight to Disaster
programme.desc: Survivors and eyewitnesses recount the moment British Midland Airways Flight 092, travelling from London to Belfast, ran into difficulties in January 1989. The Boeing 737-400 crashed onto the motorway embankment between the M1 and A453 near Kegworth, Leicestershire. Rare archive footage and expert analysis uncovers the chain of decisions that sealed the fate of 47 passengers and injured dozens more

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon and guests examine the candidates performances and analyse the task, the boardrooms and the firings. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programme.desc: Part two of two. Xi Jinpings government have high hopes for Joe Biden, Donald Trumps successor as US president, but these are dashed after a frosty meeting in Alaska. Biden sparks further anger in Beijing when he signs a nuclear submarine deal with Australia and the UK, and takes on China over tech, before Nancy Pelosis visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer than ever to military confrontation

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Dangerous Dogs: Is The Ban Working? - Panorama
programme.desc: Two years ago, the Government banned XL Bully dogs, promising it would save lives. Reporter Sam Poling investigates whether that promise has been kept. Attacks are still happening, the police say the law is difficult to enforce, and some animal charities say they are overwhelmed with abandoned dogs. Sam hears from families whove lost loved ones and owners struggling to stay on the right side of the law

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary prepares for her official debut in London society, buoyed by her friendship with Ann and the attentions of Mr Ryder. But a run-in with her old rival Caroline Bingley threatens her growing confidence. Mary faces up to her old rival at a dinner party, while an urgent message from home throws everything into uncertainty

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary rushes to Pemberley, fearing her mother is gravely ill, only to be thrown back into the demands of family life. She begins to lose hope of ever returning to London, until Mr Ryder unexpectedly turns up. His clear admiration for Mary helps the family to recognise how much she has changed. As excitement mounts over Mr Ryders visit, Mary finds herself on the brink of a momentous decision

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie heads to Kent, where he meets soon-to-be-grandmother Tracey and suggests to her that borrowing a pet might lower her blood pressure. He explains how spending time with animals has been proven to boost happiness, improve wellbeing and lower the risk of dying from cardiovascular disease

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martel and Nitro from Gladiators
programme.desc: Martel and Nitro from Gladiators duel with each other over ideas at a studio flat in London, and theres a look back into the archives, all the way back to 2009, when Martin Roberts was charmed by a Grade II listed house in Devon

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary and Angelina Bakalarou work together to repair a lamp left to visitor John by his friend Brian who passed away during the Aids epidemic. He has fond memories of the lamp decorating Brians flat during his lavish cocktail parties in the 1980s and is hoping the experts can bring it back to its former glory. Chris from Surrey brings in his father Chriss shoes used when he was a training partner to Roger Bannister when he broke the four-minute mile. Sisters Sara and Simone bring in a brooch left to them by their step-grandmother Jean who died in a plane crash in 1973 for Richard Talman to take a look at. Mark Stuckey is tasked with repairing a reel-to-reel audio recorder that holds performances by visitor Michaels father from before he died

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: King of Kings (1961)
programme.desc: Biblical epic chronicling the life of Christ, from the rumours of a coming Messiah before his birth, through his rise as a spiritual leader to his eventual betrayal, crucifixion and resurrection. Narrated by Orson Welles and starring Jeffrey Hunter, Siobhan McKenna, Robert Ryan, Rip Torn, Hurd Hatfield and Harry Guardino

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Mark Horton visits Liverpool to view Brunels famous ship, the Great Eastern, which now lies beneath the mud. Mark recounts the story of her greatest triumph - how she conquered the Atlantic by connecting Britains edge to America with 2,000 miles of telegraph wire

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin (1966)
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deightons character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents the show from Eston in Cleveland. Among the treasures unearthed are a cubist elephant by Eduardo Paolozzi, used to promote floor covering, a Victorian painting bought for five shillings and the first commercially produced toy robot, made in the 1930s

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts value objects brought into Blackburn Cathedral, where Charles Hanson assessing a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip Serrells eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills in the final round of challenges on double points Friday

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: The team returns to offer more horticultural tips and advice, with Carole Baxter and Brian Cunningham kicking off the season with a range of jobs that can be done now. Carole also planting up an Easter container that will bring colour and joy to any garden, while Brian starts some much needed maintenance-pruning on the stem bank border. Plus, a trip to Edinburgh to catch up with what George Anderson is doing in his own garden in Joppa

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again, and only one can go through to the final. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges - Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is award-winning Belfast director Lisa Barros DSa, whose films Ordinary Love and Good Vibrations have already been celebrated in dishes earlier in the week

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: With a long Easter weekend to look forward to, Monty Don is on hand with practical gardening advice and inspiration, including how to get potatoes in the ground if you fancy growing them. Hell also be sowing seeds for a colourful cut flower display, and starting a new project to show how easy and inexpensive it is to create topiary from yew cuttings. Ashley Edwards heads to a magical spring garden in Horsham to share his love of the season of renewal while, in Carmarthenshire, theres a grower who gave up her job in London to follow her dream of becoming a flower farmer

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Change sweeps across Xudum, and every big cat character must adapt to survive. The core of the Xudum lion pride has moved south, but a group of its young, opportunistic females has remained in the north. The crew is excited to find one of these lionesses has cubs, but as a first-time mum, she has a lot to learn. Cheetah mum Pobe experiences tragedy when a leopard attacks, but hope returns when her three-year-old daughter Neelo is found, and shes thriving, while Lediba the leopard reveals there is more to her than meets the eye, but shes under increasing pressure to catch large prey

programme.title: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)
programme.desc: A New York stockbroker rises from unemployment to become one of the richest men in the citys financial elite through an illegal scheme to defraud investors. His wealth brings him an outrageously debauched lifestyle, but comes at a price as the FBI become suspicious of his manipulation of the markets. Martin Scorseses fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Jonah Hill and Matthew McConaughey

programme.title: The Beasts (2022)
programme.desc: Middle-aged French couple Antoine and Olga have moved to an inland Galician hamlet, happy to live off the land and renovate some dilapidated properties and intending to settle down. However, their presence inflames two locals to the point of shocking violence. Thriller, starring Marina Foïs, Denis Menochet and Luis Zahera. In French and Spanish

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again, and only one can go through to the final. The guest judge is award-winning Belfast director Lisa Barros DSa, whose films Ordinary Love and Good Vibrations have already been celebrated in dishes earlier in the week

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: As the team launches high-powered rockets into the sky on Skinwalker Ranch, they are frozen in shock by what they encounter

programme.title: In Search of Aliens
programme.desc: Experts contemplate whether an advanced civilisation of giants built Maltas mysterious megaliths, with the native people having only limited involvement. Presented by Giorgio Tsoukalos

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In the 21st century, UFOs have gone from fringe science to mainstream obsession. This programme examines societys proximity to understanding the strange events in the sky

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: Jake Herak and his hounds let loose on a black bear that leads them up a gruelling course of falling rock and treacherous terrain, while Mike Horstman makes a life-threatening climb up and down the cliffs of Kodiak Island leading to a battle with a brown bear

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les makes a major deal with a new supplier for a truckload of TVs, before getting excited about rare Detroit Tigers baseball memorabilia

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les has stainless steel jewellery coming to the shop, but Ashley refuses to sell it - until she is forced to do so by her cousin Karen

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Cousin Karen is ready to shake things up on the showroom floor, but Ashley is less than thrilled to have someone invading her turf

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Blackburn to help members of the public sort their treasures from their trash. Antiques dealers Michael Hogben, Mark Stevens, Stewart Hofgartner and Corrie Jeffrey try their best to buy their favourite items, but its a Jaeger-LeCoultre Atmos clock that causes a stir in the den today

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Host David Dickinson and antiques experts David Hakeney, Tim Hogarth and Jo Brayshaw visit Bradford, West Yorkshire, where they inspect some diamond and white gold jewellery and Victorian epaulettes

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.sub-title: Theres No Crying in Pawn Stars
